parameters.name: device
parameters.label: Device
parameters.description: A menu of available audio devices to output to. Selecting

parameters.name: file
parameters.label: Sound File
parameters.description: , , , or audio file. The file can be mono or stereo.

parameters.name: datlist
parameters.label: DAT List
parameters.description: Links to a which can be used as list of audio files to choose from. When using the , the first input (Input 0: Triggers) on the can contain mulitiple channels to fire the files in the list individuall

parameters.name: volume
parameters.label: Volume
parameters.description: 0 = mute, 1 = full volume. Using the second input (Input 1: Volume) or the python method, will override the value of this parameter.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Determines how the audio is triggered when using the first input to trigger.

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.label: Trigger
parameters.description: Triggers the audio to play.

parameters.name: cookalways
parameters.label: Cook Every Frame
parameters.description: Forces to cook every frame.

parameters.name: stereo
parameters.label: Stereo Mode
parameters.description: Sets output to just 2 channels, front left and front right.

parameters.name: frontleft
parameters.label: Front Left
parameters.description: Play audio on this output based on mapping table above.

parameters.description: Play audio on this output based on mapping table above.

parameters.description: Play audio on this output based on mapping table above.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.label: Specifydevice
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: errormissing
parameters.label: Errormissing
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

examples.label: Configure Time Slice and Specifydevice
examples.code:
n = op('audioplay1')
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.specifydevice = True
n.par.exportmethod = 'autoname'
examples.description: Common settings from 4 real Derivative examples
